reasoning: To break up the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA) requires an enormous structural and legislative effort, as NOAA's core functions and existence are established by law. While the Trump administration's FY2026 budget proposal released in April 2025 included steep funding cuts for NOAA and proposed dismantling its research arm (likely leading the Tracker to mark it as "in progress"), completing the full break-up by July 1, 2025, is virtually impossible. Congressional approval would be necessary to completely dismantle the agency, and the FY2026 budget will not be finalized until at least late 2025. Additionally, Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick explicitly disavowed the Project 2025 goal of breaking up NOAA during his January confirmation hearings, introducing further friction. The maintainers of the Project 2025 Tracker (such as /u/rusticgorilla) are known for strict, legally accurate tracking; they will not mark a massive reorganization as "complete" merely based on a budget proposal. By July 2025, the objective will inevitably remain "in progress."
